Hello!👋 My name is Vincent and I'm a senior Computer Science major and Data Science minor at Luther College, graduating in 2025 with a 3.895 GPA. I'm a software developer and have a strong passion to learn and grow in this fast paced industry. I love working on personal projects in my free time and finding new and up incoming technologies to play with. Outside of programming, I enjoy physical exercise and, recently, playing the game Go. My professional experience spans AI development and full-stack web development. At Mayo Clinic, I built full-stack web applications integrating deep learning models, collaborating closely with a dynamic team to meet project goals. At Leum Tech, I developed ASP.NET web applications, rapidly mastering technologies like MVC programming and RESTful APIs. I’m skilled in Python, Django, PyTorch, and Hugging Face, with hands-on experience in Docker, CI/CD, and microservices. I’ve also led personal projects like Rizzopedia, a sentiment analysis web app, and an automated video editing tool for Skol Norse News

### Demand Forecasting Algorithm
...
Developed a robust demand forecasting algorithm using Gradient Boosting and Facebook Prophet on Azure Databricks. Leveraged historical data for trend modeling, elasticity curves, and baseline estimations to forecast demand over time. Integrated MLOps practices for seamless model deployment and monitoring. Utilized Python and PySpark for data preprocessing, feature engineering, and scalable processing across large datasets. Automated ETL pipelines to ensure efficient data flow and real-time updates, optimizing forecast accuracy and enabling better decision-making for business operations. Show less
...
Analyzed reasons behind losing opportunities and provided actionable recommendations to drive improvement. Extracted data from Salesforce and other sources, processed it using ETL pipelines in Alteryx, and performed feature selection using RFE and feature importance techniques. Built and evaluated multiple machine learning models, including Random Forest, SVM, Decision Trees, and Boosting techniques, using metrics such as precision, recall, and F1-score. The key recommendation was to focus on leads with a score above 0.70, resulting in a 65% increase in lead conversion rates. Show less
...
Predicted fraudulent credit card transactions using machine learning models. Balanced classes with the SMOTE technique and deployed multiple models, selecting Random Forest based on key performance metrics.
...
To reduce customer churn, telecom companies must predict high-risk customers, particularly in the prepaid model, where churn prediction is critical. This involves understanding the three phases of churn—good, action, and churn—and predicting the likelihood of high-value customers churning. Multiple machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Random Forest, and SVM, were employed to enhance prediction accuracy. Class imbalance was addressed to ensure robust model performance and reliable insights. Show less

Hi, I am Samyak Jain, a Computer Science Engineering graduate from Birla Institute of Technology, Mesra, currently working as an AI Developer at Fastenal. I bring a strong foundation in computer science fundamentals, data structures, and algorithms, along with hands-on experience in Data Science and Generative AI. At Fastenal, I have been contributing to impactful AI-driven projects, including the development of a Generative AI chatbot "BLUE" and an AI-based Quoting Sourcing tool, showcasing my ability to apply cutting-edge technology to solve real-world challenges. A couple times this week I got to the end of my work day and had to force myself to stop because I was in the middle of something that was interesting, challenging, made me tap into my creativity or just gave me the feeling I was learning something new.
   Getting laid off was hard and I would never wish the experience on anyone.  But looking back there are parts of it that gave my opportunities.  I got the chance take classes that were interesting and at times pushed me (never ask your college to let you take more than the allowed credits during a summer session).  I started a new job with a company that I enjoy.
   And my new position has exposed me to new tools (Fabric, AI agents) and allowed me to use skills that I learned during my time off like Python and Statistics (discovered in that class that much of what I had been doing for years actual had names!).
   I guess I was realizing this week that I'm grateful for where I am now.  Of course there are still challenges, but I'm feeling recharged and able to face them.

results.title: Jenna Williams
highlights:
Senior Data Visualization Analyst at [Fastenal](https://www.linkedin.com/company/fastenal)
...
I recently graduated from Winona State University with a B.S. in Data Science, specializing in Management Information Systems (MIS) and a minor in Computer Science. I am passionate about transforming raw data into meaningful insights that drive informed decision-making. With a strong foundation in data analytics, predictive modeling, and business intelligence, I enjoy tackling complex problems and developing solutions that optimize efficiency and performance. My experience spans multiple industries, including my roles as a Business Intelligence Intern at Michael Foods Inc., where I leveraged Tableau and RPA to enhance reporting and automation, and as a Data Consultant for Semcac, where I analyzed survey data to help inform strategic planning. Additionally, in my role as a Student Lead IT Specialist, I provided hands-on technology support and maintained documentation to streamline IT operations. I am proficient in SQL, JMP, Tableau, Excel, R, Python, and Java, with expertise in data visualization, clustering, and text mining. My background in Management Information Systems (MIS) and Computer Science equips me with the technical and business acumen to bridge the gap between data and decision-making. I am currently seeking opportunities in business intelligence, data analytics, and business systems analysis, where I can apply my skills to deliver data-driven solutions that create value. Let’s connect! I’d love to discuss data-driven strategies, emerging technologies, and opportunities to collaborate.

I’m proud to share that I’ve accepted a full-time position at Fastenal as a Business Data and Strategy Analyst. I’m grateful for the experience and growth during my time at Fastenal part-time, and I’m excited to get started and continue building on it!
...
I would like to give a recap of how much my life improved over 2025. First, my team took 2nd in BBBS Insights for MUDAC 2025. My team and I grew very close and learned so much from each other. Next, I would like to highlight the part-time job I landed at Fastenal on the Business Data and Strategy Analyst team. So far, I have learned a lot about data's actual impact on a business. I also researched AI tools within Microsoft Fabric and explored ways to incorporate them into our business processes. I have also been able to meet with stakeholders directly and use Fabric to help replace and automate old processes. With the AI research, I was able to give a presentation at our annual BI Summit. At Winona State, I also had the opportunity to present on how impactful AI can be for students. Overall, it was a very good year and I hope this year is even better.

results.title: Brian Keeling
highlights:
Brian Keeling is an experienced Business Intelligence leader located in Winona, MN. With 22 years of service at Fastenal in the industrial distribution and supply chain sector, he has undertaken various roles in Operations, Human Resources, Sourcing, Product Development, Business Development, Business Analytics, and Pricing. Drawing on over 15 years of experience in developing leaders and managing large, internationally diverse teams, he ardently supports Fastenal founder Bob Kierlin’s philosophy that organizational success is contingent upon all members working towards a common goal. In his current capacity as the Director of Business Intelligence, Mr. Keeling oversees the vision and execution of Pricing, Costing, and Margin Services aimed at maximizing equitable value for Fastenal and its local leaders, accelerating sales growth, and enhancing customer experiences. Comments from Brian: In everything I do, I believe that success and fulfillment come from creating a culture of connected growth, where we challenge each other to go beyond the status quo. By following the disciplines of a learning organization, leveraging technology, and remaining nimble, the value and integrity of a company can flourish. With intention and guidance, I aim to foster educated and data driven decisions in the pursuit of value for our stakeholders and customers. Every industry’s future will bring with it a variety of challenges. In today’s environment more than ever, business success requires critical thinking and creativity, as well as the drive to learn and have systematic vision for growth.

As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. Angi is seeking an exceptional Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning to be a driving force behind Angi's transformation.
